THE DARK TOWER OF CABILAR.author: Michael Ashton and Lee Sperry
THE DARK TOWER OF CABILAR.description: A vampire has the royal crown — and you've got to get it back.

ASSAULT ON EDDISTONE POINT.author: Patricia Nead Elrod
ASSAULT ON EDDISTONE POINT.description: What happened to the signal tower? What waits for you in the misty mountains?

GRAKHIRT'S LAIR.author: John, Nephew
GRAKHIRT'S LAIR.description: The leader of a norker uprising is free. Go find him!

THE ELVEN HOME.author: Anne Gray McCready
THE ELVEN HOME.description: A brief encounter with an unusual dwelling.

INTO THE FIRE.author: Grant and David Boucher
INTO THE FIRE.description: The solution to a 15-year-old mystery lies in a volcanic mountain range. The last force sent in was destroyed. You're next.

GUARDIANS OF THE TOMB.author: Carl Smith
GUARDIANS OF THE TOMB.description: A silent forest, a lonely shrine, and no survivors.

THE TITAN'S DREAM.author: William Todorsky
THE TITAN'S DREAM.description: (ADD 5-9) A casual visit to a living, nightmare theater.

IN THE DWARVEN KING'S COURT.author: Willie Walsh
IN THE DWARVEN KING'S COURT.description: (ADD 3-5) A thief prowls the dwarven palace, but even more goes on than meets the eye.

CAERMOR.author: Nigel D. Findley
CAERMOR.description: (ADD 2-4) A highland town faces a greater danger than can be imagined — and no one wants your help against it.

THE KEEP AT KORALGESH.author: Robert B. Giacomozzi and Jonathan H. Simmons
THE KEEP AT KORALGESH.description: (DD 1-3) Only the Keep survived the destruction of Koralgesh, but few adventurers will survive the terrors that now stalk the lost Keep's halls.

FALCON'S PEAK.author: David Howery
FALCON'S PEAK.description: (ADD 1-3) Lord Falcon is dead, so no one will mind if you hunt for his treasure — you hope.

BLOOD ON THE SNOW.author: Thomas M. Kane
BLOOD ON THE SNOW.description: (ADD 3-7) In the frozen wastes, one can find wealth, beauty, and one's own death.

THE DEADLY SEA.author: Carol and Robert Pasnak
THE DEADLY SEA.description: (ADD 4-7) A rescue mission into the green depths of the ocean.

THE BOOK WITH NO END.author: Richard Emerich
THE BOOK WITH NO END.description: (ADD 8-12) A vanished tome, a faded legend, and the end of the world.

KINGDOM IN THE SWAMP.author: John Nephew
KINGDOM IN THE SWAMP.description: (ADD 6-9) Would you trust a thief to lead you to a vampire?

ESCAPE FROM THE TOWER OF MIDNIGHT.author: Paul Kane
ESCAPE FROM THE TOWER OF MIDNIGHT.description: (ADD 2-4) Two thieves' guilds fight to the death — with you in the middle.

FLUFFY GOES TO HECK.author: Rick Reid
FLUFFY GOES TO HECK.description: (ADD 3-5) Yes, cute widdle Fluffy has been dognapped, and you must brave the flames of Heck to save her.

TROUBLE AT GROG'S.author: Grant and David Boucher
TROUBLE AT GROG'S.description: (ADD 1) Can't a half-ogre find a little peace in a riverside town full of adventurers, bigots, and thieves?

THE ROTTING WILLOW.author: Edward P. Bromley III
THE ROTTING WILLOW.description: (ADD 7-9) This roadside stop could well be your very last.

LADY OF THE LAKE.author: Laura Ferguson
LADY OF THE LAKE.description: (ADD 1) Even ladies in distress aren't all that they seem.

THE STOLEN POWER.author: Robert Kelk
THE STOLEN POWER.description: (ADD 1-3) A missing tome, a trail of clues, and an unusual cult.

THE KAPPA OF PACHEE BRIDGE.author: Jay Batista
THE KAPPA OF PACHEE BRIDGE.description: (ADD OA 2-5) An unusual visitor who likes villagers — medium rare.

THE TROUBLE WITH MYLVIN WIMBLY.author: Andrew McCray
THE TROUBLE WITH MYLVIN WIMBLY.description: (DD 1-3) A midnight encounter with a halfling guerrilla and a vengeful magic-user.

THE EYES OF EVIL.author: Tom Hickerson
THE EYES OF EVIL.description: (ADD 10+) A promise of adventure and riches, with a hint of total annihilation.

HIRWARD'S TASK.author: Rich Stump
HIRWARD'S TASK.description: (ADD 4-7) Hirward the Wizard has a little problem on his hands — and it's destroying his fortress, followers, and future.

AFTER THE STORM.author: Nick Kopsinis and Patrick Goshtigian
AFTER THE STORM.description: (ADD 8-10) The strangest things wash up on the beach sometimes.

WHITE DEATH.author: Randy Maxwell
WHITE DEATH.description: (ADD 4-7) One reason why they call it 'the dead of winter'.

BRISTANAM'S CAIRN.author: John Nephew
BRISTANAM'S CAIRN.description: (ADD 8-12) A friendly little cottage and a dreadful secret.

HOUSE OF THE BROTHERS.author: Mark R. Shipley
HOUSE OF THE BROTHERS.description: (ADD 6-10) Two huge opponents from the WORLD OF GREYHAWK™ Fantasy Setting.

FORBIDDEN MOUNTAIN.author: Larry Church
FORBIDDEN MOUNTAIN.description: (ADD 4-7) A mad venture across the fourth dimension.

TORTLES OF THE PURPLE SAGE.author: Merle and Jackie Rasmussen
TORTLES OF THE PURPLE SAGE.description: (DD 4-10) Part 1 of an Expert-level quest into a hostile wilderness.

NIGHTSHADE.author: Nigel Findley
NIGHTSHADE.description: (ADD 1-3) A 'simple errand' is never what it seems.

TORTLES OF THE PURPLE SAGE.author: Merle and Jackie Rasmussen
TORTLES OF THE PURPLE SAGE.description: (DD 4-10) Part 2 of an Expert-level trek to three lost cities.

THE MATCHMAKERS.author: Patti Elrod
THE MATCHMAKERS.description: (ADD 1-3) All's fair in love and rivalry.

SAMURAI STEEL.author: Daniel Salas
SAMURAI STEEL.description: (ADD-OA 3-5) A beautiful face hides treachery and deceit.

THE JINGLING MORDO CIRCUS.author: Vic Broquard
THE JINGLING MORDO CIRCUS.description: (ADD 10+) Step right up, ladies and gentlemen.

MOUNTAIN SANCTUARY.author: John Nephew
MOUNTAIN SANCTUARY.description: (ADD 1-3) Obnoxious little problems can easily become obnoxious big ones.

FOR A LADY'S HONOR.author: Estes Hammons
FOR A LADY'S HONOR.description: (ADD 4-7) When the going gets tough, the thieves get going.

IN DEFENSE OF THE LAW.author: Carl Sargent
IN DEFENSE OF THE LAW.description: (ADD 7-10) They sure don’t make lawful allies like they used to.

THE WOUNDED WORM.author: Thomas Kane
THE WOUNDED WORM.description: (ADD 4-8) Crippled, wingless, old — and a thousand times more dangerous.

THE FLOWERS OF FLAME.author: Jay Batista
THE FLOWERS OF FLAME.description: (ADD-OA 5-8) Why are your governor, your Emperor, and your gods so interested in a fairy tale?
